message.abstract:
<jats:title>ABSTRACT</jats:title>
                <jats:p>The RNA extraction is an important checkpoint for the detection of SARS-CoV-2 in swab samples, but it is a major barrier to available and rapid COVID-19 testing. In this study, we validated the extraction-free RT-qPCR method by heat-treatment as an accurate option to nucleic acid purification in Algerian population.</jats:p>
title: Improved COVID-19 testing by extraction free SARS-Cov-2 RT-PCR

reference.unstructured: Taipale J. and Linnarsson S. To stop COVID-19, test everyone, repeatedly. Medium (2020). Available at: https://medium.com/@sten.linnarsson/to-stop-covid-19-test-everyone-373fd80eb03b.

Corman, V.M. , et al. Detection of 2019 novel coronavirus (2019-nCoV) by real-time RT-PCR. Euro Surveill 25 (2020).

Susan Sefers and Jonathan E. Schmitz (2013). Molecular Contamination and Amplification Product Inactivation. Tang, Y-W and Stratton CW (Eds). Advanced techniques in diagnostic microbiology. (pp. 505-525).
Ioanna Smyrlaki et al. Massive and rapid COVID-19 testing is feasible by extraction free SARS-CoV-2 RT-qPCR. BioRxiv. Available at: https://doi.org/10.1101/2020.04.17.20067348

reference.article-title: Limits of Detection of 6 Approved RT-PCR Kits for the Novel SARS Coronavirus-2 (SARS-CoV-2)
